  "findings": [
    "PVC flue pipe is back-pitched, preventing proper drainage of acidic condensate back to the furnace trap.",
    "Risk of condensate pooling in the vent pipe, which can block flue gases, cause pressure switch faults, or leak into the living space."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-slope the PVC flue piping to ensure a minimum upward pitch of 1/4 inch per foot toward the furnace.",
    "Verify all pipe hangers and supports are properly spaced to prevent future sagging."
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched condensing furnace vent violates code and manufacturer specifications. It causes condensate to pool, which restricts flue gas flow, risks carbon monoxide spillage, and leads to premature equipment shutdown due to pressure switch failures.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54"
  ]
}
